title: Ring-AllReduce, Choosing Batch Size, and TensorBoard GPU Profiling
description: How GPUs communicate, how to pick the right batch size, and how to profile your training runs to find bottlenecks.
tags: Communication Primitives: Broadcast, Scatter, Gather, Reduce
How to Choose the Right Batch Size
GPU Profiling with PyTorch Profiler and TensorBoard
Optimizing a Training Run: From Profile to Fix

notebooks.title: Ring-AllReduce Simulation
notebooks.objective: Simulate ring-allreduce in pure Python, compare it with naive allreduce, visualize the scatter-reduce and allgather phases step by step, and analyze bandwidth scaling to understand why ring-allreduce is optimal.

notebooks.title: Batch Size Experiments
notebooks.objective: Train models with different batch sizes, apply linear and square root learning rate scaling rules, measure the importance of warmup, analyze gradient noise, and find the practical batch size sweet spot.

notebooks.title: GPU Profiling with PyTorch Profiler
notebooks.objective: Use torch.profiler and record_function to capture detailed training timelines, analyze CUDA kernel breakdowns, compare DataLoader configurations, benchmark mixed precision, and identify compute vs memory vs communication bottlenecks.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
